name: Gradient Descent
def: Iteratively minimises a differentiable function by stepping opposite the gradient.
problem: Fit model parameters by minimising a loss function over (often huge) datasets.
mechanism: Compute the gradient of the loss and update parameters by −learning_rate·gradient; stochastic/mini-batch variants estimate it from data samples.
limits: No convergence guarantee for non-convex loss
whenNot: Tiny convex problems with closed forms
myths: "Gradient descent finds the global minimum" — only guaranteed for convex objectives

complexity.Note: O(params · iterations); per-step cost = gradient eval
